us-politics.headline: US Rescues Downed Airman as Iran War Grinds On
us-politics.body:
The US military rescued an airman shot down over Iran, with Trump announcing the operation publicly. A retired Army general predicted the conflict could wrap up in under 60 days, though that timeline remains contested and unverified.

The war is already hitting American wallets. Jet fuel costs have skyrocketed, forcing airlines to cancel flights and raise fares. A former World Bank chief warned Iran's economy is collapsing into a barter system under the pressure of sustained conflict and sanctions.

The rescue offers a rare piece of good news amid a costly and uncertain campaign. How long the fighting lasts - and what it ultimately costs - remains the central question.

sources.title: US rescues missing service member in Iran: Trump
Former World Bank chief says Iran economy collapsing into ‘barter system’ amid conflict
Retired Army general predicts Iran war wrapped up in ‘less than 60 days’
Jet fuel costs skyrocket amid Iran war, exacerbating crisis for airlines, travelers
Trump says U.S. military has rescued airman shot down over Iran

us-politics.headline: Judge Blocks Trump Push to Police College Admissions by Race
us-politics.body:
A federal judge has blocked the Trump administration's effort to require colleges to prove race played no role in admissions decisions. The ruling halts a data-collection push that critics called a backdoor enforcement mechanism following the Supreme Court's 2023 ban on affirmative action.

Supporters of the administration's move argued it was a legitimate step to enforce existing law and ensure universities comply with the court's ruling. Opponents said the requirement was vague, burdensome, and designed to intimidate institutions rather than enforce anything specific.

The ruling is a setback for Trump's broader push to reshape higher education policy, though the administration is expected to appeal.

sources.title: Federal judge blocks Trump push to collect race-based admissions data
Judge blocks Trump bid to require colleges to prove race not considered in admissions

us-politics.headline: ICE Tactics, Immigration Clash Fuel Partisan Flashpoints
us-politics.body:
Black Democrats in Congress have pushed back against ICE agents wearing face masks during operations, drawing comparisons to historical uses of masked enforcement to intimidate communities. Republicans and DHS have defended the practice as a safety measure for agents.

A separate dispute erupted after Democratic Senator Chris Van Hollen claimed an undocumented immigrant caused a crash while fleeing ICE. DHS disputed his account directly, saying the facts did not support his framing. Both sides are using the incident to press their broader arguments on immigration enforcement.

The fights reflect a wider pattern - immigration enforcement tactics are now a daily political battleground, with each incident quickly absorbed into competing narratives.

sources.title: In fight against ICE facemasks, Black Democrats point to history
DHS slams Democrat Sen Chris Van Hollen claim, says illegal alien caused crash while fleeing ICE

us-politics.headline: Election Control, Orban, and Harris: The Political Backdrop
us-politics.body:
A new ad campaign backed by Republicans is targeting Trump's push to federalize US elections, warning it would strip states of control over their own voting systems. The effort signals that opposition to some Trump policies is not confined to Democrats.

Abroad, Trump and Putin are both backing Viktor Orban ahead of Hungary's election, according to the Washington Post. Vice President Vance has been linked to outreach efforts. Critics say the alignment raises questions about US foreign policy priorities.

Meanwhile, Fox News flagged that Kamala Harris is traveling and speaking in ways that suggest a 2028 presidential run. Harris has not confirmed any plans. The speculation is early, but the political positioning has already begun.

sources.title: New ad campaign speaks out against Trump’s threat to ‘federalize’ elections
In Hungary election, Trump and Putin are backing Viktor Orban
Kamala Harris' travels and comments clearly point to 2028

au-politics.headline: Australia scrambles to secure fuel supply amid shortage fears
au-politics.body: Japan and other Asian nations have assured Australia that fuel supplies will continue normally, as Prime Minister Albanese works to head off a domestic shortage. The assurances follow a period of acute concern about supply chains, with the crisis already biting at the bowser. Nearly 100 NSW service stations have been fined $1,100 each for displaying misleading prices during the crunch, while Western Australia has moved to expand its FuelWatch scheme, ordering all regional stations to report prices from May. Farmers are among the hardest hit. One grower told the ABC the cost of fuel is making it difficult to put food on the table. The government is under pressure to show it has the crisis under control before it deepens further.

sources.title: 'Struggling massively': Farmer finds it hard to put food on the table
Nearly 100 NSW service stations fined $1,100 each over misleading petrol prices amid fuel shortage crackdown
Asian countries assure Australia ‘normal supply’ of fuel will continue as Albanese focuses on averting shortages
Japan assures Australia will get 'normal supply' of fuel
All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands

au-politics.headline: Sisters of slain officer to sue Victoria Police for negligence
au-politics.body: The sisters of Constable Neal Thompson, killed by Dezi Freeman, plan to sue Victoria Police for negligence, their lawyer has confirmed. They allege police failed in their duty of care and say the family feels abandoned by the force. Freeman shot Thompson during a confrontation that drew intense scrutiny of police decision-making and risk management protocols. The lawsuit will test whether Victoria Police can be held liable for the circumstances that led to Thompson's death. The case is likely to reignite debate about officer safety, mental health support for police families, and institutional accountability. Victoria Police has not yet publicly responded to the planned legal action.

sources.title: Sisters of officer killed by Dezi Freeman plan to sue police for negligence, lawyer says
'Abandoned': Sisters of officer slain by Dezi Freeman to sue Victoria Police

au-politics.headline: Murder arrest at Melbourne airport; Tasmania Police shooting
au-politics.body: A man was arrested at Melbourne Airport while attempting to board an international flight, and has since been charged with murdering a woman. Police intercepted him before he could leave the country. Separately, a man is in serious condition in Tasmania after being shot by police in Goodwood, south of Hobart. Tasmanian authorities have not released details about the circumstances that led officers to discharge their weapons. Both incidents are under investigation.

sources.title: Man arrested attempting to board international flight at Melbourne airport charged with murdering woman
Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

au-politics.headline: Fatal crashes claim girl, 7, as highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle
au-politics.body: A seven-year-old girl has died after an ATV rollover in Adelaide's north-east, one of two separate crash fatalities reported on the same day. The deaths come as travellers celebrated the reopening of a major highway closed by Cyclone Narelle, with many Australians taking to the roads over the Easter long weekend. A magnitude-5.5 earthquake also rattled a remote APY Lands community in South Australia early on Sunday, described by locals as a sharp morning wake-up call. No injuries were reported from the quake.

sources.title: 7yo girl dies after ATV rollover in Adelaide's north-east
Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle
Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' to remote community

world.headline: US-Israel War on Iran Enters Day 37 With Escalating Strikes
world.body:
US and Israeli forces continued strikes on Iran for a 37th consecutive day, while Iran hit back by attacking power and water infrastructure in Kuwait, widening the conflict across the Gulf. A US F-15E was downed over Iranian territory, but both pilots were rescued - Trump called the second recovery 'miraculous.' The war is polling badly at home, with drivers already blaming Trump as fuel prices surge.

Iran is selectively easing pressure on Gulf shipping, announcing Iraqi vessels may pass the Strait of Hormuz freely. Yemen's Houthis meanwhile claimed a cluster missile strike on Ben Gurion airport. US satellite firm Planet Labs announced it would black out imagery of the conflict zone, raising concerns about transparency as Trump signals he wants an exit that avoids the appearance of failure.

sources.title: US pilot from downed F-15E plane rescued in Iran: What we know
What we know so far about rescue of US airman in Iran
Kuwait’s power, water plants damaged as Iran keeps attacking Gulf states
Thousands rally in Iraq against ‘senseless’ US-Israel war on Iran
Trump seeks to end the war without it becoming ‘an abject failure’
Iran war: What is happening on day 37 of US-Israeli attacks?
Yemen’s Houthis claim cluster missile attack on Israel’s Ben Gurion airport
‘Miraculous’: US rescues second pilot downed in Iran, Trump confirms
Trump’s unpopular war on Iran
Iran says Iraqi ships can pass Strait of Hormuz as transits tick up
US satellite firm Planet Labs announces blackout on war on Iran images

world.headline: Ukraine Drones Hit Russian Oil Refinery and Port
world.body:
Ukraine struck a Russian oil refinery in Nizhny Novgorod and a port facility in Primorsk, with fires and fuel leaks reported at both sites. The attacks mark a continued Ukrainian push to degrade Russian energy and logistics infrastructure deep inside Russian territory.

The strikes come as the broader war grinds on with no ceasefire in sight, and as global attention remains split between the Russia-Ukraine conflict and the expanding US-Israel war on Iran.

sources.title: Ukraine hits port in Russia’s Primorsk, oil refinery in Nizhny Novgorod

world.headline: Canadian Astronaut Hansen Becomes First to Fly to the Moon
world.body:
Jeremy Hansen has become the first Canadian to travel to the moon, joining NASA's Artemis program as it pushes crewed lunar missions forward. Hansen's inclusion marks a milestone for Canada's role in international space exploration under its partnership with NASA.

The mission reflects a broader acceleration of lunar ambitions more than five decades after Apollo, with multiple nations and private actors now competing for presence beyond Earth orbit.

sources.title: Jeremy Hansen is the first Canadian to head to the moon

world.headline: Israeli Forces Use Dog to Attack Palestinian Man in Mosque
world.body:
Footage circulating online shows an Israeli military dog attacking a Palestinian man inside a mosque, drawing widespread condemnation. The incident adds to mounting documentation of Israeli military conduct in Palestinian territories during Holy Week, a period already marked by heavy movement restrictions on Palestinian Christians in the West Bank and Gaza.

Critics argue the restrictions and incidents like the mosque attack are part of a systematic campaign against Palestinian life and institutions, including sustained pressure on UNRWA, the UN agency providing humanitarian relief in Gaza.

sources.title: Footage shows Israeli military dog attacking Palestinian man inside mosque
Israel’s attack on UNRWA is central to its genocide of the Palestinians
Under Israeli restrictions, Palestinian Christians mark quiet Holy Week

ai.headline: AI Creep Erodes Creative Work and Human Skill
ai.body:
A folk musician named Murphy Campbell found himself targeted by AI-generated fakes of his music, then hit with copyright trolling when he tried to fight back. His case illustrates how AI tools are being weaponized against the very creators they supposedly serve. Meanwhile, a growing backlash is pushing platforms and buyers to demand proof that work is human-made, with new logos and certifications emerging for "AI-free" creative content.

On the skills side, a widely-shared essay argues the real danger of AI isn't job loss but comfortable drift - people slowly stop understanding what they're doing as machines handle more of the thinking. Grammarly's own story adds texture: its AI-review feature reportedly flagged its own marketing copy as low-quality. The tools are eating their makers.

sources.title: Grammarly’s sloppelganger saga
The threat is comfortable drift toward not understanding what you're doing
A folk musician became a target for AI fakes and a copyright troll
Really, you made this without AI? Prove it

ai.headline: Anthropic Adds Fees as AI Coding Tools Multiply
ai.body:
Anthropic confirmed that Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra to use OpenClaw, its integration with external coding environments. The move signals that AI coding tools are entering a tiered, monetized phase after an era of bundled access. Costs are rising just as competition intensifies.

Andrey Karpathy published a public "idea file" for an LLM wiki, offering a rare glimpse into how a leading AI researcher organizes speculative thinking. Separately, a detailed breakdown of coding agent architecture lays out how retrieval, planning, and execution components fit together in production systems. Microsoft's Copilot branding chaos - one analysis counts a bewildering number of distinct products sharing the name - suggests the industry is still struggling to explain what these tools actually are.

sources.title: LLM Wiki – example of an "idea file"
Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
Components of a Coding Agent
How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'?

ai.headline: Hardware Friction Slows Software: Linux, GPUs, and Repair Rights
ai.body:
An AWS engineer reported that Linux 7.0 cuts PostgreSQL performance roughly in half, with no easy fix in sight. The regression affects a workload that millions of production systems depend on, and kernel maintainers have not yet identified a clean solution. Infrastructure teams running recent kernels should treat this as urgent.

On the hardware access front, Apple approved a driver letting Nvidia external GPUs work with Arm Macs, a small but meaningful win for users locked out of GPU upgrades. Less encouraging: tech companies are actively lobbying to gut Colorado's right-to-repair law before it takes full effect. And Germany's eIDAS digital identity rollout will require users to have an Apple or Google account, handing two American platforms a mandatory role in European civic infrastructure.

sources.title: AWS engineer reports PostgreSQL perf halved by Linux 7.0, fix may not be easy
German implementation of eIDAS will require an Apple/Google account to function
Tech companies are trying to neuter Colorado’s landmark right-to-repair law
Apple approves driver that lets Nvidia eGPUs work with Arm Macs

btc.headline: Bitcoin Stalls at $67K as Bearish Sentiment Peaks
btc.body:
Bitcoin is grinding sideways around $67,000 while bearish social media chatter hits a five-week high, according to Santiment data. Sentiment has soured to its worst levels since the Iran war began, with traders openly calling new price lows a matter of time.

The pessimism is broad but not necessarily a death sentence. Historically, peak negative sentiment has preceded reversals, and some analysts argue the lack of capitulation selling keeps the floor intact. Still, with no clear catalyst on the horizon, bulls are struggling to make a case for a near-term breakout.

sources.title: Bitcoin holds steady as sentiment hits worst levels since Iran war began
New Bitcoin price lows 'matter of time' says trader with BTC stuck at $67K
Bitcoin bearish social chatter reaches 5-week high: Santiment

btc.headline: Crypto's Token Glut Is Quietly Killing the Market
btc.body:
Token supply is growing far faster than the value those tokens represent, and analysts are calling it an existential problem for the industry. Projects continue launching new assets into a market already drowning in supply, diluting returns and eroding confidence across the board.

The dynamic creates a structural headwind that price action alone cannot fix. Until projects tie token issuance more tightly to real utility and revenue, the flood of new supply will keep capping upside for even legitimate projects.

sources.title: Crypto faces ‘existential’ token problem as supply outpaces value creation

btc.headline: Drift Protocol's $280M Hack Was Months in the Making
btc.body:
Preliminary findings from the Drift Protocol exploit reveal the attacker spent months preparing before draining $280 million from the Solana-based platform. This was not an opportunistic hit. The deliberate, extended preparation points to a sophisticated actor who studied the protocol's mechanics carefully before striking.

The case underscores a persistent gap between how fast DeFi protocols ship code and how thoroughly they audit it. Slow, patient attackers keep finding that gap profitable.

sources.title: Drift Protocol $280M exploit took 'months of deliberate preparation'

btc.headline: Ant Group Bets on AI Agents Spending Crypto Autonomously
btc.body:
Ant Group's blockchain division has launched a platform designed to let AI agents transact directly on crypto rails, without human approval for each payment. The move positions Ant at the intersection of two of tech's hottest trends, and signals that autonomous AI spending is moving from theory to infrastructure.

The platform raises immediate questions about accountability and compliance when no human initiates a transaction. Bitcoin's parallel quantum-proofing push adds another layer of urgency - the security assumptions underpinning crypto rails are being stress-tested from multiple directions at once as the stakes grow larger.

sources.title: Ant Group’s blockchain arm unveils platform for AI agents to transact on crypto rails
Bitcoin's $1.3 trillion security race: Key initiatives aimed at quantum-proofing the world's largest blockchain

food.name: Miso-Glazed Leek and Mushroom Galette
food.description: A rustic free-form pastry filled with caramelised leeks and shiitake mushrooms bound with a white miso and crème fraîche mixture
food.tip: Freeze your butter for 15 minutes before grating it into the flour — it creates flakier layers than cubing and cutting

exercise.name: The Sunday Tempo Ladder
exercise.equipment: Running shoes, a flat route or treadmill
exercise.description: A structured run that builds through four 8-minute blocks — easy, moderate, comfortably hard, and easy again — training your body to shift gears rather than plod at one pace

learn.topic: Tektites and the Australasian Strewn Field
learn.description: Around 800,000 years ago, a meteorite impact scattered glassy debris called tektites across roughly 10% of Earth's surface — from southern China to Antarctica — yet the source crater has never been conclusively found
learn.why: It's a genuine open mystery in planetary science, and tracing the forensic logic scientists use to hunt an invisible crater will change how you look at the ground beneath your feet
